cards.front: Who sailed for Spain in 1492 and reached the Americas?
Christopher Columbus (year & achievement)
cards.back: 1492 — sailed for Spain, reached the Americas (landed in the Bahamas)

cards.front: Who led the first expedition to circumnavigate the globe (1519–1522)?
cards.back: Ferdinand Magellan (killed in the Philippines; his crew completed the voyage)

cards.front: Ferdinand Magellan (year & achievement)
cards.back: 1519–1522 — led the first circumnavigation of the globe (sailed for Spain)

cards.front: Who conquered the Aztec Empire in 1521?
Hernán Cortés (achievement)
cards.back: Conquered the Aztec Empire in Mexico (1519–1521), sailing for Spain

cards.front: Who conquered the Inca Empire in 1533?
Francisco Pizarro (achievement)
cards.back: Conquered the Inca Empire in Peru (1532–1533), sailing for Spain

cards.front: Who led the Corps of Discovery expedition (1804–1806)?
Lewis & Clark (achievement)
cards.back: Led the Corps of Discovery (1804–1806) to explore the Louisiana Purchase and reach the Pacific Ocean

cards.front: Who served as guide and interpreter for Lewis & Clark?
Who was the first Englishman to circumnavigate the globe (1577–1580)?
Sir Francis Drake (achievement)
cards.back: Second circumnavigation of the globe and first by an Englishman (1577–1580), sailing for England

cards.front: Who found the first sea route from Europe to India (1498)?
Vasco da Gama (achievement)
cards.back: First European to reach India by sea (1498), sailing around Africa for Portugal

cards.front: Who commanded the Chinese treasure fleet in the early 1400s?
Zheng He (achievement)
cards.back: Led seven massive voyages (1405–1433) across the Indian Ocean for Ming Dynasty China

cards.front: Who traveled the Silk Road to China and wrote about his journey?
Marco Polo (achievement)
cards.back: Venetian merchant who traveled the Silk Road to China (1271–1295) and served at the court of Kublai Khan

cards.front: Who explored the river and bay that bear his name in present-day New York?
Henry Hudson (achievement)
cards.back: Explored the Hudson River (1609, for the Dutch) and Hudson Bay (1610, for England) while seeking a Northwest Passage

cards.front: Who was the first European to explore Florida (1513)?
Juan Ponce de León (achievement)
cards.back: First European to reach Florida (1513), sailing for Spain; legend says he sought the Fountain of Youth

cards.front: Who was the first European to explore the Mississippi River (1541)?
Hernando de Soto (achievement)
cards.back: Spanish explorer who was the first European to document the Mississippi River (1541)

cards.front: Who explored Newfoundland for England in 1497?
John Cabot (achievement)
cards.back: Italian sailing for England who reached Newfoundland in 1497, establishing England's claim to North America

cards.front: Which explorer are the Americas named after?
Amerigo Vespucci (achievement)
cards.back: Italian explorer who recognized the Americas as separate continents (early 1500s); the Americas are named after him

cards.front: Who was the first European to see the Pacific Ocean from the Americas (1513)?
Vasco Núñez de Balboa (achievement)
cards.back: First European to see the Pacific Ocean from the New World (1513), crossing the Isthmus of Panama for Spain

cards.front: Who explored Australia and Hawaii and mapped much of the Pacific?
Captain James Cook (achievement)
cards.back: British explorer who mapped Australia, New Zealand, and Hawaii during three Pacific voyages (1768–1779)

cards.front: Which Norse explorer reached North America around 1000 AD?
Leif Erikson (achievement)
cards.back: Norse explorer who reached North America (Vinland) around 1000 AD, roughly 500 years before Columbus

cards.front: Who was the first person to reach the South Pole (1911)?
Roald Amundsen (achievement)
cards.back: Norwegian explorer who was the first to reach the South Pole (December 14, 1911)

cards.front: Who is traditionally credited as the first person to reach the North Pole (1909)?
Robert Peary (achievement)
cards.back: American explorer traditionally credited as the first to reach the North Pole (April 6, 1909)

cards.front: Who were the first to summit Mount Everest (1953)?
cards.back: Sir Edmund Hillary and Tenzing Norgay

cards.front: Sir Edmund Hillary & Tenzing Norgay (achievement)
cards.back: First confirmed summit of Mount Everest (May 29, 1953)

cards.front: Which country sponsored Columbus's 1492 voyage?
Which country sponsored Vasco da Gama's voyage to India?
Which country sponsored Magellan's circumnavigation?
Which country sponsored John Cabot's 1497 voyage?
What was the name of the land Leif Erikson reached in North America?
Who lost the race to the South Pole and perished on the return trip?
cards.back: Robert Falcon Scott (reached the pole in January 1912, a month after Amundsen)

cards.front: What body of water did Magellan name during his voyage?
What strait at the southern tip of South America is named after Magellan?
Who commissioned the Lewis & Clark expedition?
